A_intro:
Colleges of Agriculture in National Taiwan University, Taipei, and Chung
Hsing University, Taichung.

One of the five positions for advisers is in the field

-23-

of agricultural education. Dr. Donald Meaders is currently ona two-year assignment
to Chung Hsing University to advise the College of Agriculture on administrative and
curriculum problems, and to advise the relatively
A_methods:
new (three classes of graduates)
Department of Agricultural Education.

The Department of Agricultural Education has approximately 150 students
enrolled in a four-year program. The areas of the departmental program which are
receiving major emphasis by the adviser for strengthening-are pre-service (curriculum
revision and improved student teach
A_results:
ing), in-service (development of teaching materials
and policies for systematic distribution of bulletins and other aids), and research.

A major research project is underway with financial assistance from Ford
Foundation Funds granted to Michigan State University for research on International
Programs. A study is being made of a sample of the graduates of Taiwan Vocational
Agriculture Schools for the years 1950, 1955, and 1959. The project will yield
valuable information relative to the V-A School program as well as provide a means
of training the agricultural education staff in research methodology.
